title: Apple TV 4K - 64GB Wi-Fi $217, 128GB Ethernet $238 Delivered @ Amazon AU
body:
Deal: Apple TV 4K - 64GB Wi-Fi $217, 128GB Ethernet $238 Delivered @ Amazon AU

[muzeeb] $238 up to $399? That's disgusting
Thanks for the heads up OP. Bought.
[skibidigrimacmoggr67] I swear these weren't selling and were regularly half this price just before covid.
[FrugalNotStingy] This 3rd gen was released in Oct 2022 so it’s not the same model. This one’s been out for almost 4 years so it’s still old.
[skibidigrimacmoggr67] This one’s been out for almost 4 years so it’s still old.
yeah that's what I mean. 4 years is eternity for tech.
usually tech gets yearly release cycle, but apple TV just didn't sell well.
ok you might be right maybe mine is the one before this? but it looks identical. I think mine doesn't have ethernet.
no actually it's this one. 3rd Gen 4K A15 bionic. I remember they went as low as $89 I think (might have been price error) but I missed that and had to price beat next best which was around $100.
[FrugalNotStingy] @skibidigrimacmoggr67:
yeah that's what I mean. 4 years is eternity for tech.
I’ll call it ancient too.
But at the end of the day, it’s still just a TV box, no need for bleeding edge tech inside.
[skibidigrimacmoggr67] @FrugalNotStingy:
no need for bleeding edge tech inside.
yeah but worried about AppleTVOS update support already.
[FrugalNotStingy] @skibidigrimacmoggr67: That’s possible given Apple recently announced they would drop support for some iPads and Apple Watches for their Apple Intelligence focused iOS27 and watchOS27.
[skibidigrimacmoggr67] @FrugalNotStingy: I saw the video for the Apple watch update support drops. not a apple watch owner (I think the 18~24 hours battery life is just too silly) but seemed pretty bad. Ultra 1 was on the list. imagine paying $1299 or whatever for that and getting support dropped. 😂 it's only a year older than Ultra 2.
[FrugalNotStingy] @skibidigrimacmoggr67: I fall victim of the drop. Don’t really understand why my 6 year old iPad Pro is supported whereas my not yet 4 year Ultra watch isn’t.
[pinkybrain] @FrugalNotStingy:
Don’t really understand why my 6 year old iPad Pro is supported whereas my not yet 4 year Ultra watch isn’t.
Because Apple wants you to buy a new watch
[AustriaBargain] Just wait eight or so years for the world to restructure itself around manufacturing high density memory, then prices will drop.
[pinkybrain] Just wait eight or so years for the world to restructure itself around manufacturing high density memory, then prices will drop.
8 or so years from now most people won’t have jobs and will just be used to power the AI simulated world
[Khisanth] OMG! I've been checking out this model for the last fortnight wondering why it was so expensive, with rumours of a new version later this year. Thought I might wait to see what Prime Day brought, but no longer! Thanks for the post - also bought.
[kizzaaa] 14 day returns on this and Prime day starts 7 July so there's a short window where you could return and buy one at a potentially lower price.
Camels suggest it hasn't dropped much in the past tho.
[tries] yeah I bought one this morning without hesitation after seeing the price increases.
Sucks cause I have been waiting over a year for the new one to come out but if it's going to cost $400+ not worth waiting
[justtoreply] For a non-apple person, is this useful?
I'm getting FOMO reading this thread!
[OGGazelle] Fantastic if you use Plex (one for the best media devices for it), and probably twice as good UI as Google/Android TV for regular streaming services. If you don’t care for UI and just use steaming services, maybe you can get away with a chromecast
[justtoreply] Are there apps for channel 10 etc? My LG does not natively support 10, and also I think channel 9 app exists but now requires a newer TV than mine
[Khisanth] Yes there is. Much more fluid than on my LG too.
[RedPanda] Just wish it allowed correct Lossless audio pass through for plex. Still holding out for a new model, then I’ll retire the Shield
[tp0] It supports 8 channels of lossless audio. It also supports Atmos for all streaming services and movie purchases.
[RedPanda] @tp0: I know but It does not pass through Dolby TrueHD, Dolby TrueHD + Atmos, DTS-HD MA, DTS:X
[tp0] @RedPanda: It can decode TrueHD and DTS-HD to lossless audio. It’s just the height channels that are lost.
[HaydosK] New model won't do anything new. It will just be faster. They aren't going to pay for the license for those audio codecs or pass it though
[pinkybrain] @HaydosK: Probably it will have more power for Siri AI due to IOS 27. Some features may not work
Expected Hardware Upgrades
-A17 Pro (or Newer) Chip:
-Custom N1 Connectivity Chip
https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=a5cjBG6s6IY
[Alx75] What do you mean "regular streaming services have twice as good UI"? If you mean other streaming apps, they have the same UI and functionality as on Android TV (most of them are pretty crap, convoluted and confusing) and Apple TV won't make them any prettier. No need for this box to run other apps if your TV already supports them. As for "one of the best media devices for Plex", my 5 year old Sony TV runs Plex app just fine and it passes through every audio format correctly to my amp, unlike Apple TV.
[hessels92] Its the same thing, people love how well optimised the Apple Tv is, Clean UI with less lag when app switching etc
[OGGazelle] Was about to post this too!
Similarly waiting for next gen version (100% has Siri AI, this one won’t), but gladly secure this at $238 and eventually move to my second tv if a sale comes on for next gen in ~6-12months
[Owgasm] I’ve been waiting for it to go sub $180 for over 6-7 months…. Or for the new one…
Tragic
[FrugalNotStingy] Wanted to wait for the new one but I reckon I’ll just get this. The price hike is ugly.
[hopper] Can this also be used as hub? Got a used homepod mini to work as hub for me home bridge
[OGGazelle] Yep! Also a matter hub (or thread as well on 128gb Ethernet version)
[Owgasm] Yeah, I need it for Meross our garage after they removed hub support off iPads.
[skibidigrimacmoggr67] these are really old.
got 64GB base model for the household close to $100 after price beat. this was years ago.
I think rumors say Apple about to bring the next one out real soon. apparently it was made for "ai" or whatever so they delayed the release from last year.
[Rkel403] I have the first phone “made for AI” and it’s “AI” never arrived except for some dog sh1t image stuff that is useless. And as of their latest AI announcements this phone won’t have any of the feature … Don’t believe any of their marketing nonsense
[skibidigrimacmoggr67] yes they flopped the first version that was supposed to merged with chatgpt. it was the dumbest thing ever with the whole rainbow border. just marketing slop. embarassing.
the latest apple ai is something else not sure. but it's not meant to be a full ai like gemini/chatgpt. it's meant to be a much smarter version of siri (which absolutely sucked). one new feature I saw was like photo smart ai removal, like gemini/google phone does currently. you can remove your glasses, add to the picture in all direction to make photo larger.
I'm just mentioning the latest version of it, is the reason they delayed the latest Apple TV (it's been a very long time since they released a new model). they probably want to release all these ipads and new iphone and new apple TV all together since they are all going to support the new (non chatgpt) version of ai.
[kizzaaa] Thanks, got one. No way would I pay the RRP of $399.
Been waiting for the 4th gen to be announced for over a year now.
[Gambo] same. been waiting forever. price changes today have forced the hand. maybe that way their plan… Hmm
[Lionheart] Thanks OP, grabbed one as well for my bedroom TV, love the other one I have
[andresampras] I first bought the Amazon gift card for $200 with $5 bonus.
Brought price down to $233 for me
[Owgasm] Just did the same and cancelled previous
[zagadka] Deal makes sense but, there is supposed to be a new Apple TV later this year (September 2026?)
The current Apple TV models were designed in 2022 ish
[zagadka] If the new one is $399 in September then these old ones will be $217 again priced to clear etc
[skibidigrimacmoggr67] these old ones will be $217 again priced to clear etc
I would think so too. once the new one out with 4+ years more modern tech, nobody is going to want these old ones anymore unless they sell them very cheap. but even buying very cheap you might be looking at 2 years of upgrade cycle.
[andresampras] I suppose this price rise is Apple's shot across the bow re. imposing pricing for all new products.
So the new one in september will be at least $399 if not more.
[mrpickle] I've been waiting for the gen 4 update for the last 2.5 years. Around every October there's news to say it will be announced by November and it never came to be.
[congo] Got the 2x Apple TV Gen 1 in 2020 from JB ($159).
Is 3rd gen worth thre upgrade?
I was waiting for Gen 4….
[skibidigrimacmoggr67] no wait for the new one. if you can deal with the price increase.
upgrade support for buying this one now, is not going to be very long.
[skibidigrimacmoggr67] should be releasing real soon.
I tell this to anyone who willing to pay more, if the new one is about to be released it's going to be better value over the years and no need to worry about upgrading for the longest period.
even better if you still have Apple GC leftover from the yearly promo, or have Apple GC balance saved up by buying during 20x points (flybuys etc). works out to be about 10% back in points (which you can easily spend on groceries at your own pace). I bought about $100~$200 a year and added to Apple balance to save up for the new iPad ( I already bought it, 11th gen. 10th gen was covid rip off price $799 for base ipad so I skipped).
[Wizard] 128GB OOS.
Pass since it can't play media files via usb.
[n1x] Be aware the remote controller of these apple tvs are so unreliable and annoying
Mine keeping double jumping when navigating menus or seeking in videos. On left and right touch swipe is ok and on up and down tactile press button is okay, or the other way around. Replacing one costs about $90, third party cheaper remotes don't support Siri
I bought the Ethernet version back in 2022 for $213
[zagadka] You can use the older style Apple remotes, don’t have to use the crappy new touch pad ones
Also you can usually just use your regular TV remote (don’t need to use Apple remote at all)
[skibidigrimacmoggr67] I've had issues with touch on both. just needed to get used to it.
it's awkwardly small and thin I think that's why.
[PopCounty] Get a silicon cover for the remote. You would feel the difference. Slips won’t happen making it sturdier for your gestures.
[skibidigrimacmoggr67] @PopCounty: yes that definitely as well. aluminum very slippery and it's thin so makes it awkward to hold when you try to move your thumb to swipe the touch.
I just got used to it somehow. but same experience with both remotes.
[PopCounty] @skibidigrimacmoggr67: Yeah. The day I got silicon cover, can never go back without. It's hugely underrated in terms of experience. Good luck.
[skibidigrimacmoggr67] @PopCounty: it's ok I never actually dropped it.
could keep it in my hand, trouble getting used to it was swiping accurately with it in your hand. can't remember now I'm used to it, but I think it would move left to right and front to back or become diagonal in your hand when swiping. I guess using your thumb is the main reason because your palm kind of changes shape.
hard to explain but when you first try swiping it's like "WHO designed this!". 😂
[Randolph Duke] Be aware the remote controller of these apple tvs are so unreliable and annoying
Not my experience at all. Can also use your iPhone instead but I find that a bit more fiddly.
[illusion99] Same so many issues with the Apple remote
[stratbargain] you can turn off swipe and use it as a normal remote. that's what i do
[illusion99] Turned off swipe and still get lagged input and double jumping
[Pricebeat] Never had an issue and I have it since day 1.
[2BsQzrbgkL] This is probably the last chance at this price until the AI bubble pops
I like an optimist believing the savings will get passed on when memory prices come down again.
[AustriaBargain] Just like how we went back to vacuum tubes after the transistor bubble popped.
[Steven123] After they kill steamio, I kinda dont even touch this much
[hessels92] Nvidia shield is still king for Nuvio/Stremio
[PopCounty] It’s available via AppStore. Have you checked recently? Been using for more than 7/8 months I guess.
[hessels92] Stremio has been taken down, If you got it while it was up you got to keep it. There is a fake version up at the moment. Also Nuvio has gone so far past stremio I couldn't go back
[PopCounty] Stremio is still working for me on iPhone and Apple TV as I had it previously. Yeah just noticed, this seems to have been taken down from AppStore though. So you are right.
[Pricebeat] I use Omni on it. Find it better than Stremio
[Alx75] A lot of people in this thread going full on FOMO… In my opinion, anyone who already has an Android TV doesn't need this. The 3rd party streaming apps look and function the same on both platforms and if you want to watch Apple Shows just download the Apple TV app on your Android TV (box).
[Wokstar] When your android TV is unreliable, crashes all the time and slow you actually prefer and need this.
[Alx75] I've never experienced a crash on my Android TV, and all the apps run smoothly. Your experience may be different. I see Apple TV as a streaming box, nothing more. So the extra stuff, like the ability to play Apple Arcade on it, and the iPad level processing power (just to switch between apps) has no value to me. From that perspective, my point still stands. If you already have a TV with Android TV built in, there's really no need for this and the only thing you'll be "missing out" on is the "prestige" of owning an Apple product.
[JackFrost] Nah these are rock solid. 90% of TVs interfaces suck.
The price is the fomo.
New model will arrive this year and the old ones will be back down once again. only cop if you cant wait.
[bAps] perfect timing - bought a new LG C6 from last week's deal and the LG UI is awful.
[browncamry] I have a couple of these in storage and this price increase is making me want to start using them again
[SkeetSkeet] I've never wanted an Apple TV until now. I'm full OzBargain brain rotted
[akanub] How's it compare to the shield pro for non apple ecosystem users?
[tp0] It’s 4 years old instead of 10 years old.
Much better performance. Accurate Dolby Vision. HDR10+ support. Much better movie store.
Generally better apps from the major streamers. Best quality video for the best quality streaming service (Apple TV+).
[Alx75] The "A15 Bionic" chip that's in Apple TV came out in 2021. "Tegra X1+" that's in Nvidia Shield Pro came out in 2019. Not sure where did you get 10 years vs. 4 years old. As the other comment mentioned, Apple TV 4K does not support direct bitstream passthrough for several major high-end home theatre lossless audio formats (Dolby TrueHD, Lossless Dolby Atmos, DTS-HD Master Audio, DTS:X).
[tp0] The X1+ is a die shrink. It’s still the same X1 from 2015.
It doesn’t need to passthrough. It losslessly decodes up to 8 channels to LPCM.
It’s a common misconception that there’s no support for any lossless audio. It’s not correct.
[lonewolf] It doesnt do Lossless HD Audio Codecs. Shield pro is still king for most features etc.
[mlburnian] You could get these for 120 bucks on marketplace
[mrpickle] Thanks for the PSA.
Been waiting years holding out for the new model but just bought on officeworks to avoid the price increase.
